title.en: Udyam (MSME) Registration — Free Process, Real Benefits and the 45-Day Payment Weapon

answerBox.en: Udyam registration is free, online at udyamregistration.gov.in, needs only Aadhaar + PAN, and issues instantly — yet most small businesses and freelancers skip it and lose its biggest benefit: the MSMED Act right to be paid within 45 days, with compound interest at 3× the RBI bank rate enforced through MSME Samadhaan councils. Add priority-sector lending, collateral-free CGTMSE loans, and government-tender preferences.

en.title: Check you qualify — you probably do
en.summary: Micro/small/medium by investment + turnover; services count.
en.detail: Classification (composite): **micro** — investment ≤ ₹1 crore and turnover ≤ ₹5 crore; **small** — ≤ ₹10 crore / ≤ ₹50 crore; **medium** — ≤ ₹50 crore / ≤ ₹250 crore. **Services qualify** — freelancers, agencies, consultants, traders (for most benefits) included; proprietorships, partnerships, LLPs, companies all register. Practically every reader of this page fits micro.

en.title: Register — free, official, 30 minutes
en.summary: Aadhaar + PAN on udyamregistration.gov.in; beware paid clones.
en.detail: On **[udyamregistration.gov.in](https://udyamregistration.gov.in)** (the ONLY official portal — lookalike sites charge for the free process): authenticate with **Aadhaar OTP**, add **PAN/GSTIN** (data auto-pulls from ITR/GST), classify your activity (NIC codes), and the **Udyam certificate with QR** issues instantly. No documents to upload, no fee, no renewal — annual figures sync from your filings. Our ₹499 assistance exists for those who want it done-with-you, but the process is genuinely DIY-friendly.

en.title: Use benefit #1: the 45-day payment right
en.summary: MSMED Act overrides contracts; Samadhaan enforces it.
en.detail: Registered micro/small suppliers must be paid within **45 days of acceptance** — regardless of the PO's "90-day terms". Delay triggers **compound interest at 3× the RBI bank rate**, claimable via **[MSME Samadhaan](https://samadhaan.msme.gov.in)** facilitation councils (free online filing; buyer appeals need 75% pre-deposit). Buyers also face tax disallowance (Section 43B(h) — delayed MSME payments disallowed as expense) and disclosure duties — leverage that finance departments respect. Full recovery playbook: our [unpaid invoice guide](/help/money-recovery/unpaid-invoice-business-dues).

en.title: Stack the rest of the benefits
en.detail: Registration unlocks: **collateral-free loans** under CGTMSE guarantee (banks lend against the guarantee, not your house), **priority-sector lending** classification, **government tender benefits** (EMD exemptions, price preference, 25% procurement target from MSEs), interest-subvention and state subsidy schemes, ISO/patent-fee reimbursements, and electricity/stamp concessions in several states. Banks and marketplaces increasingly ask for the Udyam number as standard vendor KYC — registration is becoming the default business ID.

rows.label: Udyam registration
rows.note: Official portal; beware paid clone sites

en.footnote: Date matters: MSMED protections attach to supplies made while registered — register today so every invoice from tomorrow carries the 45-day/3× armour.

en.q: I'm a solo freelancer with no "enterprise". Can I register?
en.a: Yes — service-providing individuals/proprietors register as micro enterprises with just Aadhaar + PAN; lakhs of freelancers hold Udyam certificates precisely for the 45-day payment right and easier business banking. Your name is the enterprise name if you have no brand.

en.q: Is there any fee or renewal for Udyam?
en.a: None — registration is free and permanent, with annual self-declaration/auto-sync of figures. Any site charging for "Udyam registration" is an agent (at best) — the official portal is udyamregistration.gov.in. Reclassification happens automatically if you grow across thresholds.

en.q: Does the 45-day rule really override a signed 90-day payment contract?
en.a: Yes — Section 15 MSMED Act caps agreed credit at 45 days for registered micro/small suppliers; longer contractual terms are void to that extent. Interest at 3× bank rate compounds monthly from day 46 (Section 16), and the buyer cannot even deduct it as expense. Councils enforce exactly this daily.

en.q: Will registering affect my taxes or add compliance?
en.a: No new tax or filing burden — Udyam is an identity/benefit registration, not a tax registration. It pulls data FROM your ITR/GST rather than adding returns. GST registration remains governed by its own thresholds independently (see our GST guide).

en.q: What is CGTMSE collateral-free credit in practice?
en.a: Banks lend to MSEs with the CGTMSE trust guaranteeing large portions of the exposure (up to ₹5 crore cover) — so loans go through without property collateral. Approach the bank citing CGTMSE; approval still tests viability, but "no collateral" stops being an automatic no.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
